sections.title: Credits
sections.notes: Publication credits; included for completeness but not relevant for RAG.

sections.title: Clanbook
sections.anchor: ## C     l     a     n     B     o     o     K     : ™
sections.notes: Title page marker; not relevant to lore.

sections.title: Contents
sections.notes: Table of contents; establishes chapter order but contains no prose.

sections.title: Introduction: Kissing Cousins
sections.notes: Opening fiction establishing the tone and themes of the clanbook, focusing on family dynamics, the Proxy Kiss, and the disturbing nature of Giovanni Embrace practices.

sections.title: Kissing Cousins
sections.notes: Continuation of opening fiction, showing the disturbing family gathering and Primo's attempted assault on Kay.

sections.title: Before History
sections.notes: Introduction to Giovanni history, covering their mortal origins and early practices.

sections.title: Mortal Roots
sections.notes: The Giovanni's origins as a mortal merchant family in ancient Rome, practicing ancestor worship and death magic (nigrimancy).

sections.title: Dis Pater
sections.notes: Early Giovanni practices and their relationship with death and the underworld.

sections.title: Fortune's Fall
sections.notes: Historical events leading to the Giovanni's transformation from mortals to Kindred.

sections.title: No Rest For the Wicked
sections.notes: The Giovanni's continued activities and growth after their Embrace.

sections.title: The Birth of Clan Giovanni
sections.notes: Augustus Giovanni's Embrace by the Cappadocian Antediluvian in 1005 and the beginning of the clan's transformation.

sections.title: Another Theory
sections.notes: Alternative accounts or interpretations of the Giovanni's origins.

sections.title: The Premascines
sections.notes: Giovanni Embraced before Augustus diablerized the Cappadocian founder. They may still exist in hiding.

sections.title: Toward the Renaissance - In Two Forms
sections.notes: The Giovanni during the Renaissance period, covering both cultural and political developments.

sections.title: Things Grow Ugly
sections.notes: The escalation of conflict between the Giovanni and Cappadocians.

sections.title: The Khazar's Diary
sections.notes: Historical document or account relating to the Giovanni-Cappadocian conflict.

sections.title: What Is the Endless Night?
sections.notes: Explanation of the Giovanni's ultimate goal: a ritual that will tear down the barrier between the living and the dead.

sections.title: Eliminating the Threat
sections.notes: The systematic elimination of the Cappadocians after Augustus's diablerie.

sections.title: Centuries of Growth
sections.notes: The Giovanni's expansion and development from the 15th century onward.

sections.title: Expansion into Other Families
sections.notes: The Giovanni's incorporation of other families (Dunsirn, Pisanob, Milliners, etc.) into the clan.

sections.title: The Dunsirn
sections.notes: The Scottish Dunsirn branch of the Giovanni, focused on trade and commerce. Currently being pressured to learn necromancy.

sections.title: The Pisanob
sections.notes: The Mexican Pisanob branch, practicing Aztec-influenced necromancy. Struggling after the maelstrom.

sections.title: Pochtli the Premascine?
sections.notes: Speculation about whether Pochtli, leader of the Pisanob, is actually a Premascine.

sections.title: The Milliners
sections.notes: The American Milliner branch, newer and less respected despite their successes. Resentful of the main family's lack of recognition.

sections.title: The Minor Families
sections.notes: Overview of the smaller Giovanni family branches: della Passaglia, Putanesca, Ghiberti, and Rosselini.

sections.title: Holiday in Cambodia
sections.notes: Reference to Giovanni activities or presence in Cambodia.

sections.title: Strange Bedfellows
sections.notes: Discussion of unusual alliances or relationships the Giovanni have formed.

sections.title: Anziani (Elders)
sections.notes: The Giovanni's elder leadership structure and decision-making process.

sections.title: Diego on Prophecy
sections.notes: Diego Giovanni's philosophical views on prophecy and the Endless Night.

sections.title: Anziani Prudente (Cautious Elders)
sections.notes: The faction of cautious elders who favor careful, measured approaches to the Endless Night.

sections.title: Anziani Appassionati (Eager Elders)
sections.notes: The faction of eager elders who want to push forward with the Endless Night as quickly as possible.

sections.title: The Premascines
sections.notes: Detailed discussion of the Premascines and their potential existence in hiding.

sections.title: The Honored Dead
sections.notes: Giovanni who have become wraiths after death, serving the clan from the Underworld.

sections.title: The Global Giovanni
sections.notes: Overview of the Giovanni's worldwide organization and family structure.

sections.title: Three Views of the Endless Night
sections.anchor: ## three viewS oF the endleSS niGht
sections.notes: Three different perspectives on what the Endless Night will accomplish, from different Giovanni.

sections.title: Cristobal Ghiberti, 13th Generation
sections.notes: Cristobal's view of the Endless Night as a revolution that will free the Giovanni.

sections.title: Diego Giovanni, 6th Generation
sections.notes: Diego's philosophical view of the Endless Night as a transformation that will resolve Kindred conflicts.

sections.title: The Ritual of Pochtli
sections.anchor: ## the ritual oF poChtli  (level two neCromantiC ritual)
sections.notes: A metaritual that allows multiple Necromancers to pool their successes when casting other necromantic powers.

sections.title: Unico Sangue e Doppio Sangue (Single Blood and Double Blood)
sections.anchor: ## uniCo SanGue e doppio SanGue (SinGle Blood and douBle Blood)
sections.notes: The Giovanni's distinction between 'single-blooded' (born and Embraced Giovanni) and 'double-blooded' (Embraced into the clan but not born into it) members.

sections.title: Infantilism
sections.notes: The psychological phenomenon of Giovanni who remain dependent on their elders, never achieving full independence.

sections.title: L'Economia Sanguinosa (The Economy of Blood)
sections.anchor: ## l ' eConomia SanGuinoSa (the eConomy oF Blood)
sections.notes: How the Giovanni approach feeding, viewing blood as an economic resource to be managed efficiently.

sections.title: Tradizione (Tradition) The Proxy Kiss
sections.notes: The Giovanni tradition of ghouling family members before the Embrace to ensure loyalty and test worthiness.

sections.title: April 4th
sections.notes: The annual celebration of the feast that led to Augustus's decision to betray his sire. Traditionally when new family members are proposed for the Proxy Kiss and Embrace.

sections.title: An Italian Primer For Foul-mouthed Necromancers
sections.anchor: ## an italian  primer  For Foul-mouthed neCromanCerS
sections.notes: Glossary of Italian terms used by the Giovanni, including slang and necromantic terminology.

sections.title: Ghostbusters It Fuckin' Ain't
sections.notes: Explanation of the Underworld, the Shroud (sudario), and how necromancy works, written in a crude, practical style.

sections.title: Spiriti (Wraiths)
sections.notes: Explanation of wraiths (spiriti): their nature, motivations, fetters (catene), and how to deal with them.

sections.title: Valentinism
sections.notes: The heretical belief that the Endless Night will destroy the Giovanni's power over spirits rather than complete it, based on warnings from Valentina della Passaglia's ghost.

sections.title: Systems: What Spiriti Can Do
sections.notes: Game mechanics for wraiths' powers, using equivalent vampire Disciplines.

sections.title: Spirit Slaves
sections.notes: Rules for the Spirit Slaves Background, allowing characters to start with bound wraiths.

sections.title: Spettri (Spectres)
sections.notes: Explanation of spectres (spettri): deranged, destructive wraiths consumed by entities of oblivion. Much more dangerous than spiriti.

sections.title: Which One's Better?
sections.notes: Comparison of spiriti vs. spettri as servants, discussing their relative advantages and dangers.

sections.title: Systems: What Spettri Can Do
sections.notes: Game mechanics for spectres' powers and how they differ from wraiths.

sections.title: The Risen
sections.notes: Wraiths who have returned to their dead bodies, creating intelligent zombies. Standard necromancy doesn't work well on them.

sections.title: Shambler
sections.notes: Statistics for brainless walking corpses, the most common type of Risen.

sections.title: True Risen
sections.notes: Statistics for intelligent Risen with complex goals and planning abilities.

sections.title: Using Necromancy on the Risen
sections.notes: Rules for how necromancy affects Risen, with only the Bone Path having significant effect.

sections.title: The Maelstrom
sections.notes: The catastrophic storm in the Underworld that destroyed the dead cities and weakened the Shroud. Current state of chaos in the lands of the dead.

sections.title: The Maelstrom and Nigrimancy
sections.notes: How the maelstrom has affected necromancy and the Giovanni's ability to control spirits.

sections.title: The Sepulchre Path
sections.notes: The most common Giovanni Necromancy path, focusing on summoning and compelling wraiths.

sections.title: Summon Soul
sections.notes: Sepulchre Path level 2: Summons a known wraith to the Necromancer's location.

sections.title: Compel Soul
sections.notes: Sepulchre Path level 3: Forces a summoned wraith to answer questions or perform tasks.

sections.title: The Bone Path
sections.notes: Necromancy path focusing on animating and controlling dead bodies.

sections.title: Apprentice's Brooms
sections.notes: Bone Path level 2: Animate corpses to perform simple tasks.

sections.title: Shambling Hordes
sections.notes: Bone Path level 3: Animate multiple corpses simultaneously.

sections.title: Soul Stealing
sections.notes: Bone Path level 4: Rips a soul from a living body, sending it to the Underworld.

sections.title: The Ash Path
sections.notes: Necromancy path that manipulates the barrier between the living and dead worlds.

sections.title: Ex Nihilo
sections.notes: Ash Path level 4: Creates objects from nothing by drawing matter from the Underworld.

sections.title: The Vitreous Path
sections.notes: Necromancy path allowing the Necromancer to see and interact with the Underworld without crossing over.

sections.title: Spirit Manipulation (Thaumaturgy)
sections.notes: Discussion of how Thaumaturgy's Spirit Manipulation path relates to Giovanni Necromancy.

sections.title: Everyone But Us Is Stupid
sections.notes: Overview of Giovanni relationships with other groups, reflecting their arrogance and isolation.

sections.title: The Camarilla
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Camarilla, bound by the Promise of 1528 to remain apolitical.

sections.title: The Sabbat
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Sabbat, who view them as traitors who destroyed an entire clan.

sections.title: Assamites
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Assamite clan.

sections.title: Brujah
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Brujah clan.

sections.title: Gangrel
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Gangrel clan.

sections.title: Lasombra
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Lasombra clan.

sections.title: Malkavian
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Malkavian clan.

sections.title: Nagaraja
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Nagaraja, a bloodline that practices necromancy and may have been destroyed.

sections.title: Nosferatu
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Nosferatu clan.

sections.title: Ravnos
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Ravnos clan.

sections.title: Setites
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Followers of Set.

sections.title: Toreador
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Toreador clan.

sections.title: Tremere
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Tremere clan, who fear their necromantic power.

sections.title: Tzimisce
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Tzimisce clan.

sections.title: Ventrue
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Ventrue clan, competing for financial influence.

sections.title: Other Troublemakers
sections.notes: Giovanni relationships with other supernatural groups.

sections.title: Mages
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with mages and sorcerers.

sections.title: Changelings
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with changelings (fata).

sections.title: Hunters
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with hunters and other mortal threats.

sections.title: The Orphic Circle
sections.notes: Giovanni relationship with the Orphic Circle, a group of necromancers.

sections.title: New Traits
sections.notes: Introduction to new Backgrounds, Merits, and Flaws for Giovanni characters.

sections.title: New Background
sections.notes: New Background options for Giovanni characters, including Spirit Slaves.

sections.title: Ghiberti Necromancy
sections.notes: The specialized Cenotaph Path developed by the Ghiberti family.

sections.title: The Cenotaph Path
sections.notes: Introduction to the Cenotaph Path, focusing on detecting and manipulating fetters.

sections.title: A Touch of Death
sections.notes: Cenotaph Path level 1: Detects ghostly touch and influence on objects or people.

sections.title: Reveal the Catene
sections.notes: Cenotaph Path level 2: Identifies fetters and can reveal the identity of connected wraiths.

sections.title: Tread Upon the Grave
sections.notes: Cenotaph Path level 3: Senses the strength of the Shroud in an area.

sections.title: Death Knell
sections.notes: Cenotaph Path level 4: Automatically senses when someone dies and becomes a wraith nearby.

sections.title: Ephemeral Binding
sections.notes: Cenotaph Path level 5: Creates artificial fetters by coating objects or people with the Necromancer's blood.

sections.title: Pisanob Necromancy
sections.notes: Aztec-influenced necromancy practiced by the Pisanob, requiring ritual trappings.

sections.title: Ritual of the Smoking Mirror
sections.anchor: ## ritual oF the SmoKinG mirror (level one ritual)
sections.notes: Pisanob level 1 ritual: Uses an obsidian mirror to see as ghosts do, with Lifesight or Deathsight aspects.

sections.title: Ritual of Teyolia
sections.notes: Pisanob level 5 ritual: Tears out a Kindred's heart and preserves the heart's blood for later diablerie. Secret ritual known only to Pochtli and his lieutenants.

sections.title: New Necromantic Rituals
sections.notes: Introduction to new necromantic rituals available to Giovanni characters.

sections.title: The Hand of Glory
sections.anchor: ## the hand oF Glory (level two ritual)
sections.notes: Level 2 ritual: Creates a mummified hand that puts all mortals in a household to sleep.

sections.title: Occhio d'Uomo Morto
sections.anchor: ## oCChio d ' uomo morto (level two ritual)
sections.notes: Level 2 ritual: Replaces one eye with a corpse's eye, permanently granting Shroudsight but reducing Appearance.

sections.title: Tempesta Scudo
sections.notes: Level 3 quick-cast ritual: Creates a circle of blood that makes spirits' actions more difficult within it.

sections.title: Bastone Diabolico
sections.notes: Level 4 ritual: Creates a club from a leg bone that drains Pathos/Angst from wraiths and does aggravated damage to zombies.

sections.title: Esilio
sections.notes: Level 5 quick-cast ritual: Opens a vortex that instantly destroys wraiths and spectres. Extremely costly, permanently reduces Humanity.

sections.title: New Merits and Flaws
sections.notes: Introduction to new Merits and Flaws specific to Giovanni characters.

sections.title: Consanguineous Resistance
sections.notes: 1-point Merit: Cannot be blood bound by anyone from the same mortal bloodline. The Giovanni are suspicious of those who manifest this.

sections.title: Proxy Kissed
sections.notes: 4-point Merit (can be taken twice for 8 points): Received the Proxy Kiss before Embrace. Grants free Potence dot and indicates proper Giovanni tradition, but creates blood bond complications.

sections.title: Sanguine Incongruity
sections.notes: 5-point Merit: Does not suffer from the Curse of Lamia, but acquires permanent cadaverous appearance. Extremely rare.

sections.title: Inbred
sections.notes: 1-5 point Flaw: Represents the effects of inbreeding common among the Giovanni. Variable severity from minor defects to gross disabilities.

sections.title: Shadow Walker
sections.notes: 6-point Flaw: Partially trapped in the Underworld, making interaction with the living world difficult.

sections.title: Single-Blood Prodigy
sections.notes: Character template: A 'single-blooded' Giovanni who embodies the family's sense of superiority and tradition.

sections.title: Hurried Embrace
sections.notes: Character template: A Giovanni Embraced quickly without proper preparation, struggling to adapt.

sections.title: Then the World Went Mad
sections.notes: Character template: A Giovanni who was Embraced during or after a traumatic event, dealing with the psychological aftermath.

sections.title: Bitter Matron
sections.notes: Character template: An older Giovanni woman who has been passed over for recognition and is resentful of the family's treatment.

sections.title: Diplomat Provocateur
sections.notes: Character template: A Giovanni who specializes in manipulating other Kindred through social manipulation and necromantic information.

sections.title: Dunsirn Bully
sections.notes: Character template: A Dunsirn enforcer who uses physical intimidation and violence to protect family interests.

sections.title: Aide-de-Camp
sections.notes: Character template: A Giovanni who serves as assistant to a powerful elder, handling their business and learning from them.

sections.title: Pisanob Grave Robber
sections.notes: Character template: A Pisanob who specializes in acquiring materials for necromancy from graves and ancient sites.

sections.title: The Degenerate
sections.notes: Character template: A Giovanni who fully embraces the clan's darkest practices and has no moral boundaries.

sections.title: Fiduciary Wizard
sections.notes: Character template: A Giovanni who specializes in financial manipulation and corporate control.

sections.title: Failure
sections.notes: Character template: A Giovanni who has failed to meet family expectations and is struggling to prove their worth.

sections.title: Cugini di Sangue
sections.notes: Section on notable Giovanni characters, 'Cousins of Blood.'

sections.title: Isabel Giovanni
sections.notes: Information about Isabel Giovanni, a notable single-blooded Giovanni character.

sections.title: Emilio Rosselini
sections.notes: Information about Emilio Rosselini, a Rosselini family member known for his cruelty.

sections.title: Claudia Dunsirn, the Grail Seeker
sections.notes: Information about Claudia Dunsirn, a Dunsirn merchant struggling with the family's demands to learn necromancy.
